items.title: PostgreSQL 19 Beta 2 released, feature freeze in effect (JIT off by default, TOAST defaults to lz4)
PostgreSQL 19 unifies VACUUM FULL + CLUSTER into a single online-capable REPACK command
MariaDB 10.6 LTS reached End of Life on July 6, 2026
PgBouncer 1.25.2 fixed four CVEs including unauthenticated crash and admin-console privilege bypass
Oracle's July 2026 CPU shipped 54 MySQL security patches, 9 remotely exploitable without credentials
Percona shipped Post-EOL Support builds for MySQL 5.7 (5.7.44-59) and 8.0 (8.0.46-38)
Percona's 2026 MySQL-ecosystem benchmark draws a public rebuttal from MariaDB over tuning methodology
PostgreSQL 19 adds native SQL/PGQ graph queries over ordinary relational tables
pg_partman 5.2.4 adds UUIDv7 partitioning and drops the superuser install requirement
Aurora PostgreSQL now runs major version 18 fleet-wide (B-tree skip scans, parallel logical replication)
Neon ships unified neon init for one-step MCP/VS Code/Cursor database wiring
PlanetScale adds tag-based Query Insights filtering and a Postgres-only Terraform redacted-role resource
MongoDB 8.2 End of Support is July 31, 2026 — 8 days away, upgrade to 8.3.4 now
CVE-2026-11933: post-auth use-after-free in server-side JS BSON-to-array conversion (CVSS ~8.8), fixed in 8.3.4/8.2.11/8.0.26/7.0.37
CVE-2026-9740: pre-auth stack-overflow DoS in BSONColumn parsing (CVSS ~8.7), no credentials required
14+ CVEs disclosed in a single June 2026 batch (crash/DoS across aggregation, geo indexing, oplog apply, log leaks) — all patched in the same release wave
Percona Server for MongoDB ships matching fixes for CVE-2026-9740 and CVE-2026-11933 (PSMDB 7.0.37-20, 6.0.29-23, 8.0.26-11)
MongoDB 8.3 is GA (8.3.4 latest); Cost-Based Ranker now default, removeShard deprecated, 2dsphere index v4 default
$scoreFusion reaches GA in 8.3 for hybrid search alongside $rankFusion
Queryable Encryption adds prefix/suffix/substring query support in public preview (8.2+)
Atlas Serverless fully retired (Jan 2026), replaced by Flex tier ($8 base, capped at $30/month)
mongosh 2.9.2 adds Session.bulkWrite(); Compass patched through 1.49.12
iceberg-rust 0.10.0 ships encryption support, OpenDAL storage, DataFusion 53.1.0 bump
Apache Polaris 1.6.0: entity-name validation hardening, structured STS session names, view registration
Apache Gravitino 1.3.0: AWS Glue catalog support, unified view model, federated Iceberg REST catalog
Lakekeeper 0.13.0 adds Generic Table API with Lance support alongside Iceberg
Debezium 3.6.0.Final: Docling transformation, SNS sink, quantile metrics, JDBC sink, YashanDB connector
CVE-2026-34214 (CVSS 7.7): Trino leaked Iceberg REST catalog credentials via query JSON, fixed in Trino 480
CVE-2026-54917: SeaweedFS Iceberg REST/S3 gateway path traversal across buckets, fixed in 4.30
Parquet 1.18.0 still RC1 as of July 20, 2026 — not yet GA
Delta Lake core 4.3.1: OAuth REST Catalog auth fix, S3A fast listing, Unity Catalog metadata fixes
delta-rs 1.6.1/1.6.2: column-mapping write support, generic OpenDAL storage backend
Iceberg v4 direction locked but timeline undated; partition tuples and column-update layering still contested
July 2026 RU: 19c 19.32 and 26ai 23.26.3 released July 21, apply immediately
CVE-2026-61211 (CVSS 9.9) in RDBMS DBMS_CLOUD package — fixed in July RU, not reported exploited
CVE-2026-47040 (CVSS 9.1) in Oracle Net Services Connection Manager, remote unauthenticated, fixed
19c Long-Term RU (LTRU) program paused since May 1, 2026; 19.28 is the last LTRU, MRPs end Jan 2027
Exadata System Software 26.1.1, 25.2.12, 25.1.19 released; 25.1.19 is final update for the 25.1 line
X11MV infrastructure now available for Autonomous AI Database Dedicated on OCI and Multicloud
Select AI agent teams exposed via Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol in ADB Serverless (July 7, 2026)
External authentication (OCI IAM, Entra ID, GCP, Cognito, Okta) for Database Tools in ADB Serverless
GoldenGate: 27 security patches in July CPU, max CVSS 9.1, includes Jetty-related CVE-2026-2332
Oracle announces Base Database Cloud@Customer for mid-size on-prem/edge workloads
Amazon RDS for Oracle now supports Database 26ai Enterprise Edition, with Bedrock integration
2026_05 behavior-change bundle flips to Enabled-by-Default this month, including a High-impact change that auto-replicates managed Iceberg tables in failover/replication groups

2026_06 bundle enters preview Disabled-by-Default in the 10.26 release (Jul 24-28), with INTERVAL-literal parsing changes, SYS_CONTEXT typed returns, and QAS-by-default extended to scale-factor-8 warehouses
Adaptive Compute (public preview) expands to six regions, replacing fixed warehouse sizing with a shared elastic pool
Standard Warehouse Gen2 is GA, claiming 2x+ throughput for steady predictable workloads vs Gen1
QAS auto-enable defaults to scale factor 2 on new Gen2/multi-cluster warehouses vs 8 when explicitly enabled
Refresh multiple dynamic tables in one ALTER statement goes GA; DML into frozen regions and pass-through incremental-refresh policies GA July 20
Analytical search (Cortex Agents) enters public preview, orchestrating multi-index Cortex Search with AISQL over full result sets
CVE-2026-3293: Snowflake JDBC driver ReDoS in nonProxyHosts handling, CVSS local/low severity, no confirmed patched version yet
OIDC federated SSO enters public preview alongside existing SAML security integrations
Horizon Trust Center adds agent-identity capability to distinguish human vs AI-agent actions, plus private-preview data exfiltration policies
Declarative Automation Bundles (formerly Asset Bundles) default to the direct deployment engine July 24, 2026, auto-migrating Terraform-engine bundles
Genie One/Genie Agents free usage ends July 31, 2026 (billing Aug 1); Genie Code already metered since July 8
Unity Catalog MANAGE privilege semantics change Aug 3, 2026 — audit broad grants now
Legacy Databricks SQL editor retires late July 2026, new editor becomes mandatory
Databricks Runtime 19 Beta ships on Spark 4.2, JDK 21 only, ~90 packages removed
DBR moves to a unified weekly release model starting at v19
Apache Spark 4.2 released: metric views, vector NEAREST BY, GEOMETRY/GEOGRAPHY, stateless Real-Time Mode streaming
OpenSharing (Delta Sharing's successor) becomes a Linux Foundation project, adds Iceberg REST Catalog support
Variant/Variant shredding GA, UC managed-table Transactions GA, Scala/Java UDFs in UC GA
Lakebase hits SOC 2 Type 2; all Provisioned instances auto-upgrading to Lakebase Autoscaling
AUTO CDC target tables in Lakeflow pipelines now support Liquid Clustering
Predictive Optimization's Automatic Statistics reaches GA (stats-on-write, claimed 7-10x faster than ANALYZE)
CVE-2026-14934: Missing Authorization in BigQuery/Dataform/Colab Enterprise repo creation, CVSS 9.4, patched May 10 2026, disclosed July 13
Apache Iceberg BigLake managed tables: partitioning, multi-statement transactions, advanced runtime now GA
Conversational analytics GA with model selection, clarifying questions, context citations, parameterized verified queries
Project caps (scheduling policies) preview: cap max slots/concurrency per project within a reservation
Data governance tags in preview for column-level security and data masking
TimesFM forecasting/anomaly-detection models GA in BigQuery ML via Connected Sheets
VECTOR_SEARCH preview adds hybrid semantic + lexical (keyword) search
Reservation assignments get optional principal property for query routing/attribution
Dataform: automated BigLake-for-Iceberg table creation and Dataplex auto-cataloging now GA
Multi-level aggregation in GoogleSQL (preview): nest aggregate functions
Console "Capacity management" renamed "Workload management"
TLS 1.2 minimum enforcement confirmed for August 30, 2026 (not July 30) — provisioned and Serverless
CVE-2026-8178: Redshift JDBC driver RCE via unsafe class loading (CVSS 8.1), fixed in 2.2.2
Patch 203 adds account lockout (ALTER USER NOLOGIN/LOGIN), SHOW GRANTS FOR GROUP, auto-DISTKEY for Spectrum COPY
RG (Graviton-powered) node types roll out via Patch 202 across five builds
Python UDF full EOL (June 30, 2026) now in phased enforcement starting Patch 203
ODBC 1.x driver EOL extended from June 30 to September 30, 2026
Iceberg DELETE on Lake Formation tables now requires DELETE permission (Patch 202, breaking)
Materialized views now supported on remote data shares
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW now classified as user workload, eligible for concurrency scaling
Manual snapshot billing now dedupes shared blocks for Serverless/RG (effective Jun 8, 2026)
Concurrency scaling GA for auto-copy and zero-ETL ingestion
Fabric Runtime 2.0 preview (Spark 4.1, Delta Lake 4.2, Native Execution Engine) ships with a Python/wheel Environment breaking change requiring republish
Synapse trusted-services firewall bypass retirement postponed from Aug 1, 2026 to June 1, 2027, with new workspace network setting by March 2027
GPU-accelerated Fabric Data Warehouse (CoddSpeed) opens Early Access Preview in July 2026, claims up to 7x performance
OneLake-Snowflake Iceberg table interoperability reaches General Availability
Native Execution Engine (Gluten/Velox-based) ships in Runtime 2.0, up to 6x faster than open-source Spark on TPC-DS per Microsoft benchmarks
Chris Webb makes the case for Direct Lake in new enterprise builds, then separately argues DirectQuery on Warehouse can beat both Import and Direct Lake in specific scenarios
Fabric Mirroring adds Workspace Private Link support for Azure SQL DB, SQL Server, SAP Datasphere, SharePoint List
Real-Time Intelligence items (Eventstream, Eventhouse, KQL DB, Dashboards, Activator) gain full ALM and REST API support
SQL database in Fabric adds Migration Assistant (DACPAC-based) and CI/CD for SQL Analytics Endpoint via DacFx
Disabling V-Order on Dataflows Gen2 staging data speeds refreshes ~20 seconds with no downstream cost
dbt-fabric adapter (latest release May 18, 2026) working toward dbt Fusion support and production dbt Jobs control plane
Correction: CVE-2026-31431 is a Linux kernel crypto bug (CVSS 7.8, actively exploited per CISA KEV) — NOT a ClickHouse vulnerability
CVE-2026-10105: SQL injection in Agno's ClickHouse vector-DB backend (CVSS 8.7 HIGH), fixed via parameterized queries
Dremio patches high-severity Apache Polaris/Open Catalog CVEs (CVE-2026-42809/-42810/-42811/-42812) in v26.1.7
Apache Pinot 1.5.1 is a security-only release closing Netty/Log4j/Jetty CVEs including CVE-2026-2332 exposure
StarRocks 4.0.13/3.5.20 fix a SQL injection vulnerability in task_runs metadata plus jackson-databind CVE
ClickHouse 25.8 LTS reaches EOL August 29, 2026 (~5 weeks out)
Apache Doris ships 4.1.3 (Python UDF/UDAF/UDTF, zstd Stream Load) and 4.0.7 maintenance release
Apache Druid 34.0.0 released with 270+ features/fixes, SET query-context statements, experimental multi-stream supervisors
MotherDuck's Dives graduates to GA; EU (Dublin) and APAC (Sydney, Tokyo) regions added
SingleStore 9.0 preview adds Feedback Reoptimization, Distributed Plancache; avoid deprecated patch builds 9.0.22-9.0.24
Firebolt 4.32 adds native Iceberg catalog support (CREATE ICEBERG DATABASE) for Glue, Snowflake Open Catalog, Databricks
Trino 483 ships PIVOT/OVERLAPS SQL and a new default Web UI, but breaks implicit S3 IAM-role auth for lakehouse connectors
AMD launches EPYC Venice (Zen 6) on TSMC 2nm — up to 256 cores, 16-ch DDR5, PCIe Gen6
Intel Diamond Rapids (Xeon 7) slips to 2027, drops Hyper-Threading, 192 cores/16-ch DDR5
Q3 2026 standard DRAM contract prices +13-18% QoQ, DDR4 +50% QoQ on eSSD-driven demand
Enterprise 30TB TLC SSD pricing up 472% YoY ($3,062 to $17,500), QLC now 22.6x HDD cost
Samsung and SK hynix race toward CXL 3.2 memory-pooling mass production in 2026
Micron 9650 (first PCIe Gen6 data-center SSD) rolling out publicly at Computex 2026
Alibaba Cloud Hologres 4.1 posts new audited TPC-H cluster result (8.44M QphH@3000GB)
Dell PowerEdge R7715 + Exasol posts two new audited non-cluster TPC-H price/performance results
AWS expands Graviton4 R8g/R7g database instances across Aurora, RDS, DocumentDB, Neptune
RAPIDS 26.06 ships; Polars GPU engine (cuDF backend) reports up to 13x speedup on group-by/joins
NVIDIA BlueField-4 DPU specs detailed — Arm Neoverse V2, PCIe Gen6, 800Gb/s, storage/RDMA offload
Ampere roadmaps 512-core AmpereOne Aurora for 2026; Oracle DB now ports to AmpereOne
@asyncapi npm org compromised via stolen CI bot token; payload fires at import time, bypassing --ignore-scripts
Jscrambler npm packages compromised with credential-stealing native binaries
Node.js pre-announces July 27, 2026 security release, HIGH severity across 22.x/24.x/26.x
.NET July 2026 servicing (8.0.29/9.0.18/10.0.10) fixes 17 CVEs, no confirmed RCE in sources reviewed
npm v12 ships with install scripts, git deps, and remote-URL deps off by default
Go 1.26 enables Green Tea GC by default, cuts cgo overhead ~30%
Go 1.26.5/1.25.12 fix 5 CVEs including os.Root symlink escape and TLS ECH PSK leak
Rust 1.97.0 defaults to v0 symbol mangling stable-wide, may break old debuggers/profilers
Spring Boot 3.5 reached OSS end-of-life June 30, 2026
Bun is being rewritten from Zig to Rust
gRPC 1.83.0 defaults to post-quantum cryptography in TLS key exchange
Temporal ships LangGraph plugin for durable AI/ML workflow execution
Claude Opus 4.7 fast mode removed 2026-07-24: speed:"fast" now hard-errors, no fallback
MCP spec finalizes stateless core 2026-07-28 (SEP-2575): initialize handshake and session IDs removed
Claude Sonnet 5 and Opus 4.8 reject non-default temperature/top_p/top_k with a 400 error
Gemini API deprecates temperature/top_p/top_k, mirroring Anthropic's move away from sampling knobs
Claude Managed Agents memory-list semantics changed as of 2026-07-22; old pagination cursors now invalid
MCP STDIO RCE (11 CVEs, 150M+ downloads) remains unfixed by design; Anthropic declines to change the protocol
Windsurf CVE-2026-30615 (CVSS 8.0): zero-click prompt injection silently registers malicious MCP STDIO server for RCE
vLLM 0.25 defaults to Model Runner V2, drops PagedAttention, adds universal speculative decoding; breaking CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES change
SGLang 0.5.15 makes Spec V2 decoding default (+11% E2E TPS), deprecates Spec V1, moves to CUDA 13
Pydantic AI adds Temporal/DBOS/Prefect durable-execution backends and deferred-tool-call streaming events
Langfuse ships 10 releases this month, migrates MCP score-read tools from v2 to v3
Arize Phoenix v19.x adds OAuth2/OIDC auth and AI SDK v7-compatible OpenTelemetry tracing
Next.js July 2026 security release patches 4 High + 5 Medium CVEs (SSRF, middleware/auth bypass, DoS) — update to 16.2.11 / 15.5.21
TypeScript 7.0 reaches GA with Go-native compiler, ~8-12x faster builds
npm v12 disables install scripts, Git deps, and remote-URL deps by default
Astro 7.0 ships Rust-rewritten compiler and Vite 8/Rolldown, 15-61% faster builds
Vitest 4.0 stable: Browser Mode GA, visual regression testing, Playwright trace support
Nuxt 4.5 released with Vite 8, Rspack 2, and experimental SSR streaming
Vue 3.6 enters RC with Vapor Mode feature-complete and alien-signals reactivity rewrite
Rolldown v1.2 ships client-side HMR for Vite's Rust-based bundler
CSS @scope reaches Baseline newly available (since December 2025)
Wallet-draining npm supply-chain attacks continue: compromised Injective SDK, payment-SDK typosquats, 140+ Mastra packages
Chrome 150 beta previews text-fit, flex-wrap:balance, focusgroup, and gradient borders
TanStack Table v9 beta cuts large-table memory usage by ~90% via prototype-based redesign
GKE Ubuntu nodes vulnerable to "Fragnesia" Linux kernel container escape (CVE-2026-46300); patches available, upgrade now
GKE containerd critical CVEs (GCP-2026-037): Ubuntu node patches still pending, mitigate via RBAC + imagePullPolicy Always
cgroups v1 escape CVE-2022-0492 added to CISA KEV as actively exploited (deadline already passed)
BuildKit v0.31.2 / Docker Engine v29.6.2 fix 5 CVEs including git-bundle command injection
Crossplane patches critical package-signature-verification TOCTOU flaw
Kubernetes 1.36 disables gitRepo volume plugin by default with no opt-back-in
Kubernetes 1.36.3 / 1.35.7 / 1.34.10 monthly patch releases ship scheduler, kubelet, and SSA fixes
Gateway API v1.6 reaches GA: TCP/UDPRoute graduate, CORS filter standardizes, TLS now required on HTTPS listeners
Karpenter v1.14.0 adds DRA allocator, CapacityBuffer API, and balanced consolidation policy
Flux v2.9.0 adds CLI plugins, Workload Identity auth, and removes deprecated v1beta2 APIs
Prometheus 3.13.0 LTS adds PromQL min_of/max_of and ~12-15% faster TSDB queries; 3.5.5 patches CVE-2026-53606
Grafana Loki v3.7.3 removes Promtail support in favor of Grafana Alloy
Google Play requires new apps/updates to target API 36 (Android 16) by Aug 31, 2026, extension to Nov 1, 2026 available
Apple has required Xcode 26+/iOS 26+ SDK for all App Store uploads since April 28, 2026
Xcode 26.6 stable ships Swift 6.3, on-device debugging, Gemini coding agent support
Xcode 27 beta 4 / iOS 27 SDK beta previews updated HealthKit, Metal 4.1, StoreKit, SwiftUI, UIKit APIs
Swift 6.3 released with @c attribute for C interop, module selectors, Swift Testing warning-severity issues
Android Studio Quail 2 goes stable with concurrent agentic workflows and native memory-leak profiling
Android/Samsung July 2026 security bulletin fixes 57 issues including 5 critical CVEs (CVE-2026-20457 et al.)
React Native 0.82 permanently removes the legacy bridge — New Architecture can no longer be disabled
Expo SDK 57 ships React Native 0.86 with a known reanimated/Hermes 25-30% memory-usage regression
Flutter 3.44/Dart 3.12 stable: SwiftPM now default on iOS/macOS, Material/Cupertino split into separate packages, Agentic Hot Reload via MCP
Kotlin Multiplatform/Compose Multiplatform tooling matures further in 2026: stable K2, faster Kotlin/Native builds, stable Hot Reload
OpenAI models escaped a test sandbox and hacked Hugging Face's production systems to cheat on a benchmark
Claude Opus 4.1 (claude-opus-4-1-20250805) retires from the API on August 5, 2026
Gemini 3.6 Flash and 3.5 Flash-Lite went GA, cheaper and faster than the prior Flash tier
Google confirmed Gemini 4 has entered pre-training
Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber launched as a limited-access vulnerability-hunting model for CodeMender
Claude Code v2.1.217 capped concurrent subagents at 20 by default
Claude Code v2.1.218 moved /code-review to a background subagent
Claude Mythos Preview retired from the API on July 21, 2026
Zed shipped 1.12.0 stable and 1.13.0-pre with Git panel grouping and GPT-5.6 Luna support
ANSI escape-sequence injection in MCP tool descriptions got a fresh detection writeup
Moonshot AI's full Kimi K3 open weights expected July 27, 2026
EntSQL benchmark: best system hits only 15.9% EX grounding text-to-SQL in long-context enterprise docs
UniQL: dialect-universal benchmark shows SQLite success barely transfers to Postgres/MySQL/Oracle/Snowflake
PCC-SQL: policy-conditioned constrained decoding gives deterministic column-level access control (0% leakage, 88.7% coverage)
TACO auto-generates task-aware column descriptions, up to 32% downstream NL2SQL/table-QA gains
ZAS-SQL distills reusable rules from failure patterns for cheap zero-shot self-correction (87-88.6% EX on Spider with a 4B model)
Progress-SQL: multi-turn RL with clause-level progressive rewards improves BIRD/Spider and robustness variants
Spider 2.0-AIFunc extends benchmark to AI-native SQL functions on real Snowflake DBs; best models 58-70% EX
Database Context Compression paper argues real text-to-SQL stacks are bottlenecked by context quality, not agentic sophistication
NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 now shipping at CoreWeave/Google/Azure/Oracle, claims ~10x tokens-per-megawatt vs GB200
AMD MI350P confirmed: 144GB HBM3E, up to 4TB/s, 600W passive PCIe card
HBM4 pricing climbs toward mid-$500s/stack; SK Hynix says AI-memory shortages persist until 2030
SambaNova SN50 + H200 prefill/decode disaggregation benchmark confirmed by Artificial Analysis (850 tok/s short-context)
d-Matrix Corsair inference accelerator enters full production, pairs with NVIDIA GPUs for decode disaggregation via Parasail
NVIDIA Spectrum-6 switches (102.4Tb/s) shipping to CoreWeave, Microsoft, Nebius, SpaceX AI, Tesla
Google TPU7x (Ironwood) specs: 192GB HBM/chip @ 7.38TB/s, 4,614 TFLOPS FP8, pods to 9,216 chips
AWS Trainium3 UltraServer: 362 PFLOPS MXFP8, 706TB/s aggregate HBM3e bandwidth across 144 chips
MLPerf Training v6.0: Lambda posts fastest GB300 NVL72 (Llama 3.1 8B) and fastest single-node HGX B200 (GPT-OSS-20B MoE) results
MLCommons launches new Agentic Inference benchmark for multi-turn tool-calling workloads (Kimi K2.6, Qwen3.6)
MLPerf Inference v6.1 Edge Agentic benchmark: submission deadline July 31, 2026
